react.answer:
## Building a Skeleton Loader in React **Mental model:** A skeleton is a grey placeholder shaped like the content that's loading, with a moving shimmer to signal activity. Build one primitive `` (width/height/radius props) and compose it into a card layout. Swap skeleton ↔ real content on a `loading` flag. ### Step 1 — A primitive skeleton block A configurable box with a shimmer background. ```jsx function Skeleton({ width = '100%', height = 16, radius = 4 }) { return ; } ``` ### Step 2 — Animate the shimmer A gradient that sweeps via `background-position` keyframes. ```css .shimmer { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #e5e7eb 25%, #f3f4f6 37%, #e5e7eb 63%); background-size: 400% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.4s ease infinite; } @keyframes shimmer { 0% { background-position: 100% 0; } 100% { background-position: 0 0; } } ``` ### Step 3 — Compose a layout skeleton Mirror the real card's structure so there's no layout shift when content arrives. ```jsx
``` ### Step 4 — Swap on the loading flag ```jsx {loading ? : } ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Layout shift | Match skeleton dimensions to real content. | | Reduced motion | Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` to drop the animation. | | Flash on fast loads | Delay showing the skeleton ~200ms to avoid a flicker. | **Interview tip:** The point worth making is *no layout shift* — a skeleton should occupy the same box as the content it replaces, otherwise the page jumps when data lands.

vue.answer:
## Building a Skeleton Loader in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A skeleton is a grey placeholder shaped like the loading content, with a moving shimmer. Build one primitive `` (width/height/radius props) and compose it into a card. Swap skeleton ↔ content on a `loading` flag with `v-if`/`v-else`. ### Step 1 — A primitive skeleton block ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Animate the shimmer ```css .skeleton { display: block; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #e5e7eb 25%, #f3f4f6 37%, #e5e7eb 63%); background-size: 400% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.4s ease infinite; } @keyframes shimmer { 0% { background-position: 100% 0; } 100% { background-position: 0 0; } } ``` ### Step 3 — Compose a layout skeleton Mirror the card structure so there's no layout shift. ```vue
``` ### Step 4 — Swap on the loading flag ```vue ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Layout shift | Match skeleton size to real content. | | Reduced motion | Honour `prefers-reduced-motion`. | | Fast loads | Delay the skeleton ~200ms to avoid a flash. | **Interview tip:** Emphasize matching dimensions to prevent layout shift — a scoped `` component you compose everywhere keeps that consistent and avoids re-implementing shimmer per screen.

files./src/components/Skeleton.vue:
<script setup>
defineProps({
  width: { type: [String, Number], default: '100%' },
  height: { type: Number, default: 16 },
  radius: { type: [String, Number], default: 4 },
});
</script>

<template>
  <span
    class="skeleton"
    :style="{
      width: typeof width === 'number' ? width + 'px' : width,
      height: height + 'px',
      borderRadius: typeof radius === 'number' ? radius + 'px' : radius,
    }"
  />
</template>

<style scoped>
.skeleton {
  display: block;
  background: linear-gradient(90deg, #e5e7eb 25%, #f3f4f6 37%, #e5e7eb 63%);
  background-size: 400% 100%;
  animation: skeleton-shimmer 1.4s ease infinite;
}
@keyframes skeleton-shimmer {
  0% {
    background-position: 100% 0;
  }
  100% {
    background-position: 0 0;
  }
}
</style>
